title: A $30,000 Commercial. One Prompt, in Masonry.
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~31s. Featured brand: Tracksmith. Inspired by Tim Koda (DT_glUyDIP3, a one-prompt cinematic-commercial reel). Full editor brief: visual, Masonry generation, supers, edit, transitions, SFX.
concept: A capability-proof cinematic-commercial reel for Tracksmith. The inspiration claims a $30k commercial made in one prompt through a four-step workflow: structure the script with an LLM, create the project, paste and generate, export production-ready. The reframe makes Masonry the engine: a cinema-grade running commercial, pro camera work and seamless transitions, from one structured prompt. Every frame is generated in Masonry following the Make in Masonry step, then cut to the beat.
audio: Cinematic, building commercial track with a strong rhythmic drive. Audio-only: no voiceover; the on-screen text carries the workflow. Cut every super to the beat.
refsummary: The original (Tim Koda) claims a $30k Apple-style commercial made in one prompt: traditional commercials need $30k to $50k budgets, crews and weeks, but a four-step workflow replaces that, structure the ad with an LLM into a six-scene commercial, create the project, paste the structured prompt and generate a cinema-quality spot, then export production-ready in about ten minutes. The b-roll is a cinematic city runner and a wearable. Watch it for the one-prompt-to-commercial structure; our version makes the engine Masonry.
story: A capability-led commercial proof. Arc: HOOK, a $30,000-looking commercial. REVEAL, made in one prompt. CONTRAST, no crew, no $30k to $50k budget, no weeks of planning. THE WORKFLOW, structure the script, drop it into Masonry, generate cinema quality with pro camera work and seamless cuts, export production-ready in minutes. CLOSE, this is what brands actually pay for, Tracksmith x Masonry. The viewer should leave believing a cinema-grade brand commercial can come from one structured prompt in Masonry.
takeaway: Masonry can generate a cinema-grade brand commercial, pro camera work and seamless transitions, from one structured prompt, replacing a $30k to $50k crew shoot with about ten minutes of work.
script_kind: On-screen text only. This is an audio-only reel: no voiceover, no spoken lines. Every line below is a super, shown exactly as written and cut to the beat of the track. Read top to bottom, this is the complete message of the video, word for word. Clean sans; emphasize the brand word Masonry in the accent color.

script.line: this is what brands actually pay for

beats.super: Fades up over the cinematic runner.
beats.visual: Cinematic city-running hero shot, Tracksmith kit. Looks like an expensive spot.
beats.masonry: Prompt 'cinematic city running commercial, premium athletic brand, golden light, 9:16.' Generate the opener.

beats.visual: The same spot continues, but a prompt field hints it was generated.
beats.masonry: Cut to a 0.4s screen-record of one structured prompt, then back to the commercial.
beats.edit: Commercial, quick prompt flash, back to commercial.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 1 to 4: the one-prompt claim.

beats.visual: Quick contrast of an empty set versus the finished cinematic frame.
beats.masonry: Contrast beat: no crew or set, just the Masonry generation.
beats.sfx: Low drone for the cost line.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 5 to 7: the cost contrast.

beats.visual: A short screen-record structuring the ad into clean scenes.
beats.masonry: 0.6s screen-record: structure the ad concept into a clean multi-scene script, ready for Masonry.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 8 to 11: structuring the script.

beats.visual: The structured prompt pasted into Masonry; the project set up.
beats.masonry: Screen-record: create the project in Masonry, set ratio and length, paste the structured prompt.
beats.edit: Project setup, then a generated frame.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 12 to 15: create the project and paste.

beats.visual: Cinema-grade scenes render: tracking shots, the wearable, the stride.
beats.masonry: Generate the commercial in Masonry: cinematic scenes with pro camera moves.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 16 to 20: generating cinema-quality scenes.

beats.visual: Sweeping camera moves and seamless transitions across the spot.
beats.masonry: Show Masonry's camera work: sweeping moves and seamless cut transitions.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 21 to 24: pro camera work and transitions.

beats.visual: The finished spot exported, ready for the campaign.
beats.masonry: Show the export: a production-ready commercial out of Masonry in minutes.

beats.ost: this is what brands actually pay for
beats.visual: A confident hero of the finished commercial.
beats.masonry: Hero generation of the finished Tracksmith spot.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 28 to 29: the value close.

beats.masonry: Close: the Tracksmith x Masonry end card.
beats.edit: Clean cut to the end card.

notes: Editor workflow: load Tracksmith's products into the Masonry library, then generate each beat with its Make in Masonry prompt and cut per the Super / Edit / Transition / SFX direction. Demo brand is a believable stand-in; swap in the real client when producing. All visuals are generated and assembled in Masonry. No media generated yet: generation is gated on separate approval per project policy.
